name: Meetup Events and Community API
what_it_does: Meetup events and community GraphQL API for organizers and companies to create, manage, and promote in-person and virtual events to Meetup's global community of 60M+ members with RSVP management, group management, and event discovery integration. Enables AI agents to manage event creation and group management for community programming automation, handle RSVP and attendance tracking for event logistics automation, access event discovery and search for community intelligence automation, retrieve group analytics and member engagement for community health automation, manage event promotion to Meetup's member network for discovery optimization automation, handle virtual event configuration for hybrid event automation, access event photos and content management for community documentation automation, retrieve member search and group member management for community outreach automation, manage waitlist and capacity control for popular event management automation, and integrate Meetup with Eventbrite, Zoom, Google Calendar, and community platforms for end-to-end local community event automation.
use_cases: Creating local tech events from community building agents
Managing RSVPs from event logistics automation agents
Discovering relevant events from community intelligence agents
Promoting events from audience growth agents
not_for: Large-scale ticketed events with paid admission (use Eventbrite for paid ticketing at scale)
Corporate conference management (use Cvent or Hopin)
Private invitation-only events without public discovery
best_when: A developer community, startup, or organization wanting AI agents to automate Meetup group management, event creation, RSVP tracking, and community programming through Meetup's local community platform.
avoid_when: MEETUP PRO REQUIRED FOR API ACCESS: Meetup API access for group management and advanced features requires Meetup Pro subscription; automated community management via API requires Pro account; free organizer account has API limitations; automated workflows building on free API access encounter permission restrictions for group management operations. GRAPHQL API WITH EVOLVING SCHEMA: Meetup's API migrated from REST to GraphQL; GraphQL schema may have evolving fields and deprecations; automated Meetup integration must monitor GraphQL schema changelog; automated queries using deprecated fields encounter schema errors without backward compatibility guarantee. RSVP CAPACITY MANAGEMENT FOR POPULAR EVENTS: Meetup RSVP limits require active monitoring for popular events; automated event management must monitor RSVP count relative to guest limit; automated event without capacity management creates unlimited RSVP potential for venue that has physical capacity constraint.

auth.notes: Meetup uses OAuth 2.0 for API access with GraphQL API. New York, NY HQ. Founded 2002 by Scott Heiferman and Brendan McGovern. Owned by AlleyCorp / private (acquired from WeWork in 2020). Products: Local event groups, RSVP management, global member network, Meetup Pro (for organizations). 60M+ members, 330,000+ groups, 30+ countries. GDPR. Serves developer communities, fitness groups, social groups, professional networks. Competes with Eventbrite (for community events), Luma, and Facebook Groups for community event management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free member access for event discovery and RSVP; organizer account free with limited groups
pricing.paid_tiers: Organizer: $2-9.99/month; Meetup Pro: $23.99-$274/month (multiple groups, organizer tools, API access)
pricing.notes: New York NY. AlleyCorp owned. Free member access. Organizer and Pro plans. GraphQL API requires Pro for full access.

performance.rate_limits: GraphQL rate limits per OAuth client; query complexity limits; no webhooks — polling for event changes

known_agent_gotchas: GRAPHQL ONLY — NO REST API: Meetup's current API is GraphQL only (REST API deprecated); automated Meetup integration must use GraphQL query format; automated workflows designed for REST API patterns require GraphQL refactoring; GraphQL query complexity scoring applies to all requests with potential throttling for complex queries

NO WEBHOOKS — POLLING REQUIRED: Meetup has no webhook support; automated event monitoring (new RSVP, attendee cancellation) requires polling; RSVP change monitoring for event logistics automation must implement polling loop with appropriate cadence; high-frequency polling creates rate limit pressure

ORGANIZER vs MEMBER OAUTH SCOPE DIFFERENCE: Meetup OAuth scopes differ between member access (RSVP, search) and organizer access (create events, manage groups); automated event creation workflows must authorize with organizer scope; automated workflows with member-only scope encounter permission denied on organizer operations

URLNAME vs EVENT_ID IDENTIFIER INCONSISTENCY: Meetup GraphQL uses both urlname (group's URL slug) and numeric event_id in different operations; automated event management must handle both identifier formats; automated cross-group event operations must maintain mapping between urlname and event_id

MEMBER DATA PRIVACY AND GDPR RESTRICTIONS: Meetup member profile access is limited by member privacy settings; automated member data retrieval for CRM integration encounters privacy-restricted profiles returning minimal data; automated member outreach lists built from Meetup member data must respect GDPR restrictions on secondary use of member contact information
agent_readiness.security_notes: Community events. GDPR. OAuth2. US. Event, RSVP, and member community data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Community events. GDPR. OAuth2. US. Event and member data.
